prediction.fe1e978da4f84d69.actor: White House Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP) lead drafting the frontier-model pre-release vetting executive order
prediction.fe1e978da4f84d69.job: draft an executive order creating a federal working group of tech executives + government officials with a defined cyber-capability threshold (anchored on the AISI 32-step "The Last Ones" attack-range pass) above which a pre-release vetting process applies, and route the EO through interagency coordination with NIST AISI-equivalent + CISA + DHS + DOD ahead of the H2 2026 GPT-5.5 / Mythos / Opus 5 release cohort.
prediction.fe1e978da4f84d69.outcome: Published executive order with named working group, disclosure template, voluntary-test-protocol scaffolding, and a defined cyber-capability threshold mapped to AISI-equivalent benchmarks; signed and issued ahead of the H2 2026 release cohort.
prediction.fe1e978da4f84d69.motivation: Anthropic's Mythos zero-day discovery wave + the AISI 32-step cyber-attack-range pass + the UK NHS GitHub-repository lockdown have made the absence of a federal pre-release review the politically operative gap; without an EO before the H2 2026 release cohort, the administration owns the next Mythos-class incident.

task.why: Mythos Discord-leak incident + UK NHS GitHub lockdown make the absence of a pre-release-review framework politically operative; AISI 32-step pass anchors the threshold metric

prediction.fe1e978da4f84d69.actor: Anthropic + OpenAI + Google DeepMind frontier-lab government-affairs leads coordinating the voluntary pre-release vetting pilot
prediction.fe1e978da4f84d69.job: co-design a voluntary pre-release vetting pilot with the OSTP working group that maps the AISI 32-step attack-range methodology onto each lab's red-team pipeline and that publishes a summary metric (Expert-tier offensive-cyber score + Last-Ones-class attack-range pass rate) before each H2 2026 model release.
prediction.fe1e978da4f84d69.outcome: Joint pilot framework signed by Anthropic + OpenAI + Google with a published summary-metric template, an embedded AISI-equivalent red-team protocol, and a release-cycle reporting cadence aligned to the EO working-group cadence.
prediction.fe1e978da4f84d69.motivation: Voluntary co-design of the pilot framework lets the labs shape the threshold metric and reporting cadence; absent a co-designed pilot, the EO working group writes a metric the labs cannot easily satisfy and the release cycle gates on a federal-process bottleneck.

prediction.fe1e978da4f84d69.job: draft a bipartisan AI-safety-disclosure bill that codifies the EO working-group framework, gives NIST AISI-equivalent statutory authority over the cyber-capability threshold, and pre-empts the patchwork of state-level AI-disclosure regimes (CA SB 1047 follow-on, NY SHIELD-AI, Texas AI Act drafts) that emerged after the Jan 2025 Trump-EO rescission.
prediction.fe1e978da4f84d69.outcome: Bipartisan AI-safety-disclosure bill introduced in both chambers with NIST AISI-equivalent statutory authority, federal-pre-emption language for state-level AI-disclosure regimes, and a defined cyber-capability threshold tied to AISI benchmarks.
